name: rag-platform-sample
description: Sample evaluation dataset for RAG platform testing. Replace with real data for production evaluation.

samples.query: What is the maximum file upload size?
samples.expected_answer: The maximum file upload size is 20MB, which is configurable via the MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB environment variable.

samples.query: How does the document deduplication work?
samples.expected_answer: Document deduplication uses SHA-256 hash of the file content. If the same file is uploaded again, it returns a 409 Conflict. For incremental re-indexing, chunk-level hash comparison avoids re-embedding unchanged content.
The platform uses OpenAI text-embedding-3-small model which produces 1536-dimensional vectors.

samples.query: How is user data isolation enforced in vector search?
samples.expected_answer: Vector search always includes a user_id filter. When indexing, user_id is stored in the Qdrant payload. When searching, a Qdrant Filter with FieldCondition on user_id ensures users only see their own documents.

samples.query: What is HyDE and how does it improve retrieval?
samples.expected_answer: HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embedding) generates a hypothetical answer to the query using an LLM, then embeds that hypothetical answer instead of the raw query. This bridges the semantic gap between short queries and long document passages.

The reranker uses a cross-encoder model (ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2) for two-stage retrieval. First, Qdrant returns top-20 results using fast bi-encoder similarity. Then the cross-encoder reranks these by scoring query-document pairs directly, returning the top-5 most precise results.

The platform supports PDF, DOCX, TXT, and Markdown files. PDF extraction uses PyMuPDF for text and pdfplumber for tables. Scanned PDFs can be processed with OCR (pytesseract) when OCR_ENABLED is set to true.

samples.query: How are documents chunked for indexing?
samples.expected_answer: Documents use a recursive sentence-aware splitter with chunk_size=512 and overlap=50. It splits by separators in order: double newline, single newline, period+space, word boundary, then character boundary. This respects sentence boundaries.

samples.query: What rate limiting is in place?
samples.expected_answer: Rate limiting is implemented using slowapi with Redis as the backend. The default limit is 60 requests per minute per user, configurable via RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE.

samples.query: How does the streaming chat response work?
samples.expected_answer: Streaming uses Server-Sent Events (SSE). The LLM service calls OpenAI with stream=True and yields content chunks as they arrive. The conversation endpoint streams tokens in real-time to the client.
